title: MAINTENANCE-DEPTH-2
scope: Phase 49 [MAINTENANCE-DEPTH] shipped the four maintenance-workflow data models (tickets.vendor_id, sla_definitions, parts, ticket_costs) but explicitly deferred four user-facing surfaces — captured in pending-path Section A3 (A3.1 NotifyVendorOnAssignment listener, A3.2 landlord SLA admin UI, A3.3 parts:reorder-suggest cron, A3.4 ticket cost UI). Phase 54 closes all four end-to-end and adds VENDOR-ONBOARDING (landlord-initiated vendor email + signed-URL profile completion form, no User account creation — Vendor stays standalone per Phase-49 DPA-3 design). Six categories x 3 findings = 18. Cross-tenant + role enforcement at every entry point.

categories.VENDOR-NOTIFICATIONS: Phase 49 dispatched TicketAssignedToVendor event but never wired a listener. Phase 54 ships: (1) NotifyVendorOnAssignment ShouldQueue listener with Phase-16 backoff [30,60,300,1800] that sends VendorAssignmentMailable to vendor.email; (2) lang/{en,sw,ar}/maintenance.php new namespace with vendor.assigned.* keys (subject + intro + ticket-summary + cta); (3) VendorAssignmentMailable queued + locale-aware envelope reading from vendor (vendors are not Users, so no HasLocalePreference — locale falls back to ticket.landlord.locale).
categories.SLA-LANDLORD-UI: Phase 49 shipped sla_definitions table + SlaDefinitionService::resolveFor cascade but no UI. /admin/sla is super-admin scope (admin middleware = role:super_admin); landlord-scoped overrides live under /sla under role:landlord middleware. (1) SlaDefinitionController index/store/update/destroy scoped to landlord_id=$user->id (NULL global rows are read-only here — landlord can override but not edit platform defaults); (2) Pages/Sla/Index.vue Phase-31-styled table + create/edit modal showing the (category, subcategory, priority) match preview; (3) SlaDefinitionPolicy enforces landlord ownership via ownsSla helper + cache invalidation observer flushes the SlaDefinitionService cache keys on save/delete.
categories.PARTS-REORDER: Phase 49 shipped Part::belowThreshold + parts:audit-stock cron emitting parts_below_threshold_count gauge but no actionable next step. Phase 54 ships: (1) draft_purchase_orders + draft_purchase_order_lines schema (TenantScope, soft-deletes, status enum [draft|sent|cancelled], suggested_vendor_id nullable from latest ticket_parts→ticket.vendor_id heuristic); (2) parts:reorder-suggest cron daily 06:45 Africa/Nairobi (after parts:audit-stock 06:30) idempotently upserts DraftPurchaseOrder per landlord per part-batch; (3) Pages/Parts/PurchaseOrders.vue lists pending drafts with confirm/cancel actions + a visibility-only draft_purchase_orders_pending_count gauge for ops.
categories.COST-UI: Phase 49 TicketCostService::summarize returns per-category breakdown but no Vue surface reads it. Phase 54 ships: (1) Pages/Tickets/Show.vue sidebar Cost card calling summarize via a new prop; small inline SVG bar visualises parts/vendor/labor/other proportions in KES en-KE; (2) Manual cost entry modal posting to a new TicketCostController::store (landlord-only, validates category enum + amount_cents > 0 + currency=KES default); (3) TicketCostPolicy + cost-entry route gated by landlord ownership of the parent ticket + activity-log entry recorded so the cost is auditable.
categories.VENDOR-ONBOARDING: Net-new Phase 54 surface (NOT closing a Phase 49 deferral — landlord-initiated vendor onboarding to make the marketplace usable). Vendor is standalone (no vendor_user_id) so no User account creation. (1) VendorCreatedMailable sent to vendor.email on Vendor::created (Observer) with a 7-day signed-URL to /v/profile/{token}; (2) VendorProfileController::edit + ::update accepts signature middleware (no auth required) + form post updates name/phone/specialties/service_area on the existing Vendor row; vendor.email + landlord_id immutable from the URL token; (3) lang/{en,sw,ar}/maintenance.vendor_onboarding.* + Pages/Vendor/Profile.vue branded standalone form (no AuthenticatedLayout).
categories.CI: (1) Phase54MaintenanceDepth2SurfaceTest covers each closure: NotifyVendorOnAssignment listener registered + queued; /sla routes + policy registered + Vue page contains the table token; draft_purchase_orders + DraftPurchaseOrder model + parts:reorder-suggest cron scheduled at 06:45; Tickets/Show.vue contains Cost card token + TicketCostController route registered; vendor signed-URL route /v/profile/{token} exists; maintenance.md mentions Phase 54. (2) Extend docs/runbooks/maintenance.md with Phase-54 section enumerating each Phase 49 sub-scope deferral closure + the new VENDOR-ONBOARDING signed-URL contract. (3) Cross-link the Phase 49 PRD closeout entries that referenced sub-scope deferrals to the Phase 54 commits.

findings.title: NotifyVendorOnAssignment listener + mailable
findings.evidence: Phase 49 dispatches TicketAssignedToVendor (app/Events/TicketAssignedToVendor.php) but no listener subscribes. Vendors are blind to their assignments today, defeating Phase 49 VENDOR-MARKETPLACE-2.
findings.remediation: App\Listeners\NotifyVendorOnAssignment implements ShouldQueue with $tries=4 + $backoff=[30,60,300,1800] mirroring NotifyOnTicketSlaBreach. handle(TicketAssignedToVendor) sends VendorAssignmentMailable to $event->vendor->email when present (skip + Log::info when null so legacy vendors don't NPE). App\Mail\VendorAssignmentMailable ShouldQueue + afterCommit=true; envelope subject reads from __('maintenance.vendor_assigned.subject', ['ticket' => $ticket->title]).

findings.title: lang/{en,sw,ar}/maintenance.php new namespace
findings.evidence: No maintenance.php lang file exists today; all 3 locales need it for the mailable + future maintenance surfaces. Phase24CiTest i18n parity assertion identity-compares array_keys so all 3 files must mirror exactly.
findings.remediation: lang/en/maintenance.php with nested keys vendor_assigned.{subject, intro, ticket_summary, cta, fallback}; lang/sw/maintenance.php hand-translated; lang/ar/maintenance.php with [TODO-ar] placeholders matching the Phase 44 shrink-only baseline pattern (no growth past current count). Keys ordered identically across all three files.

findings.title: VendorAssignmentMailable locale resolution
findings.evidence: Vendors are not User rows, so HasLocalePreference does NOT auto-fire. The mailable must explicitly resolve the locale (fallback chain: vendor.preferred_locale if column exists, else ticket.landlord.locale, else config('app.fallback_locale')).
findings.remediation: Mailable constructor accepts Ticket + Vendor. Envelope locale resolved via App::setLocale(resolveLocale(vendor, ticket)) inside content() before view render. Or via the Mailable's locale() method that reads ticket.landlord->preferred_locale (HasLocalePreference). Document the fallback in the Mailable docblock.

findings.title: SlaDefinitionController landlord-scoped CRUD
findings.evidence: Phase 49 shipped sla_definitions cascade where landlord_id NULL = platform default. Landlords have no UI to add or edit their own overrides; they must edit DB rows directly.
findings.remediation: App\Http\Controllers\SlaDefinitionController with index (list landlord rows + display global rows read-only), store, update, destroy. All actions enforce landlord_id=$user->id on the SlaDefinition (the global rows landlord_id=NULL are NOT editable from this controller). Routes registered under role:landlord middleware: /sla GET/POST + /sla/{sla} PATCH/DELETE. NOT /admin/sla — that namespace is super-admin only.

findings.title: Pages/Sla/Index.vue table + create/edit modal
findings.evidence: Vue surface absent today. Phase-31 styling convention + Phase-51 modal pattern apply.
findings.remediation: resources/js/Pages/Sla/Index.vue table columns: Category | Subcategory | Priority | Response | Resolution | Source (landlord override vs global default) | Actions. Create + Edit Modal forms with cascade-preview pane showing which (category, subcategory, priority) tuples this row matches. Landlord rows show Edit + Delete; global rows show 'Inherited' badge with no actions.

findings.title: SlaDefinitionPolicy + cache invalidation observer
findings.evidence: No SlaDefinitionPolicy exists. SlaDefinitionService::resolveFor caches results 5min with keys sprintf('sla:resolve:%s:%s:%s:%s'); writes today don't invalidate the cache so a landlord saves an override and sees no effect for up to 5 minutes.
findings.remediation: App\Policies\SlaDefinitionPolicy mirroring VendorPolicy shape with view/create/update/delete returning user->isLandlord() && sla->landlord_id === user->id; AuthServiceProvider $policies registration. App\Observers\SlaDefinitionObserver::saved + ::deleted call SlaDefinitionService::flushCache($sla->landlord_id, $sla->category, $sla->subcategory, $sla->priority) which deletes the specific cache key + a wildcard pattern when null/category-empty.

findings.title: draft_purchase_orders + lines tables
findings.evidence: No purchasing schema exists. Phase 49 belowThreshold gauge has no actionable destination today.
findings.remediation: Migration phase54_create_draft_purchase_orders_tables. draft_purchase_orders: id, landlord_id FK, suggested_vendor_id FK nullable, status enum(draft|sent|cancelled) default draft, notes text nullable, sent_at timestamp nullable, timestamps + soft_deletes + unique(landlord_id, status, suggested_vendor_id) for the open-draft-per-vendor invariant. draft_purchase_order_lines: id, draft_purchase_order_id FK cascade, part_id FK, qty_suggested int, cost_per_unit_cents_snapshot int, timestamps. App\Models\DraftPurchaseOrder + Line models with TenantScope; STATUS_* constants + scopeDraft + scopePending.

findings.title: parts:reorder-suggest cron + suggestion algorithm
findings.evidence: Phase 49 emits the signal; no consumer.
findings.remediation: App\Console\Commands\PartsReorderSuggest scheduled daily 06:45 Africa/Nairobi (after parts:audit-stock 06:30, before tickets:audit-sla 07:00). For each landlord with parts below threshold: groups parts by suggested_vendor_id (latest ticket_parts → ticket.vendor_id, NULL when no history); idempotently upsert DraftPurchaseOrder per (landlord_id, suggested_vendor_id) with status=draft via unique constraint; syncs lines (insert new below-threshold parts, leave existing line qty alone). suggested qty = max(1, reorder_threshold * 2 - qty_available). visibility-only gauge draft_purchase_orders_pending_count{landlord_id}.

findings.title: Pages/Parts/PurchaseOrders.vue landlord UI
findings.evidence: Even with the cron running, landlords cannot review or act on the drafts without DB access.
findings.remediation: Pages/Parts/PurchaseOrders.vue lists pending DraftPurchaseOrders (per-vendor cards: vendor name + total estimated KES + line count + 'Confirm' / 'Cancel' buttons). Confirm flips status=sent + sets sent_at; Cancel flips status=cancelled. POST /parts/purchase-orders/{order}/confirm + /cancel routes under role:landlord with ownership policy. Empty-state shows a link to parts:audit-stock + parts:reorder-suggest cadence docs.

findings.title: Tickets/Show.vue Cost card + summarize prop
findings.evidence: Phase 49 TicketCostService::summarize returns the breakdown but no Vue surface reads it.
findings.remediation: TicketController::show passes a new costs prop = TicketCostService::summarize($ticket) (only when user can view costs — landlord/caretaker, NOT tenant). Tickets/Show.vue sidebar gains a Cost card after Details: small horizontal stacked bar (4 segments: parts/vendor/labor/other in proportion) + total KES formatted via Intl.NumberFormat en-KE + per-category rows. Tenant view hides the card entirely.

findings.title: Manual cost entry modal + TicketCostController::store
findings.evidence: Phase 49 auto-records parts costs via TicketResolutionService::recordParts but landlord-entered vendor/labor/other costs have no surface.
findings.remediation: App\Http\Controllers\TicketCostController::store($ticket) validates category enum in ['vendor','labor','other'] + amount_cents > 0 + currency='KES' (rejects 'parts' which is auto-recorded only). POST /tickets/{ticket}/costs. Cost card 'Add cost' button opens modal with category select + amount input (KES with cent precision) + notes textarea. On success, the card re-renders with new totals + the activity timeline logs 'cost_recorded' entry via TicketActivity.

findings.title: TicketCostPolicy + auditable seam
findings.evidence: Without a policy, manual cost entry could be abused for cross-tenant data injection.
findings.remediation: App\Policies\TicketCostPolicy: create($user, Ticket) returns landlord-owns-ticket. AuthServiceProvider registration. TicketCostController::store calls $this->authorize('create', [TicketCost::class, $ticket]). TicketActivity row 'cost_recorded' with metadata {category, amount_cents, currency} so the audit trail surfaces.

findings.title: VendorCreatedMailable + signed URL on Vendor::created
findings.evidence: Today landlords add Vendor rows with no notification to the vendor; the vendor is unaware they exist in the marketplace.
findings.remediation: App\Observers\VendorObserver::created dispatches VendorCreatedMailable to vendor.email when present. Mailable contains a 7-day signed URL to /v/profile/{token} (URL::signedRoute('vendor.profile.edit', ['vendor' => $vendor->id], now()->addDays(7))). VendorObserver registered in AppServiceProvider. Skip dispatch when vendor.email is null (some vendors are phone-only).

findings.title: VendorProfileController::edit + ::update signed-URL surface
findings.evidence: The Mailable's signed URL needs a controller pair. Vendor is standalone so no auth — signed URL IS the auth.
findings.remediation: App\Http\Controllers\VendorProfileController::edit(Request, Vendor) gated by middleware('signed'). ::update accepts name/phone/specialties/service_area updates; landlord_id + email immutable from the URL token (validation rejects mutation attempts). Routes: GET /v/profile/{vendor} edit + PATCH /v/profile/{vendor} update, both under middleware signed + throttle:vendor-profile (custom limit 20/min).

findings.title: Pages/Vendor/Profile.vue standalone form + i18n keys
findings.evidence: The signed-URL surface needs a Vue page outside AuthenticatedLayout (no nav, no user menu — vendors are not logged in).
findings.remediation: Pages/Vendor/Profile.vue branded standalone form (PropManager logo + brand colors + Phase-31 wizard styling). Fields: name (read-only-after-set), phone, specialties (multi-select chips), service_area (text). lang/{en,sw,ar}/maintenance.vendor_onboarding.* keys: title + intro + field labels + success message + signed-URL-expired error.

findings.title: Phase54MaintenanceDepth2SurfaceTest
findings.evidence: Six categories of closures need invariant assertions so a future refactor cannot silently regress any of them.
findings.remediation: tests/Feature/Phase54MaintenanceDepth2SurfaceTest with invariants per category: NotifyVendorOnAssignment listener registered for TicketAssignedToVendor + ShouldQueue + $backoff defined; lang/maintenance.php files exist in en/sw/ar with parity; /sla routes registered + SlaDefinitionPolicy registered; draft_purchase_orders + draft_purchase_order_lines tables exist + DraftPurchaseOrder model exists + parts:reorder-suggest scheduled at 06:45 Africa/Nairobi; Tickets/Show.vue contains 'Cost' token + TicketCostController::store route registered; vendor.profile.edit signed route registered + Pages/Vendor/Profile.vue file exists; docs/runbooks/maintenance.md contains 'Phase 54'.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/maintenance.md Phase-54 section
findings.evidence: Phase 49 [MAINTENANCE-DEPTH] established maintenance.md. Phase 54 inherits + extends.
findings.remediation: Append Phase-54 section with: closures table mapping A3.1-A3.4 to commits; SLA cascade landlord-override recipe; parts:reorder-suggest cadence + draft PO confirm/cancel flow; ticket cost UI walk-through; vendor onboarding signed-URL contract + 7-day TTL rationale.

findings.title: Cross-link Phase 49 PRD closeout to Phase 54 commits
findings.evidence: Phase 49 sub-scope deferrals reference 'Phase 50+' in their closeout notes but the resolution actually lands in Phase 54.
findings.remediation: Update phase-49-audit-prd.json audit_closeout notes for the 4 sub-scope items to cite Phase 54 commits. NO behavioural change — just cross-reference accuracy so future readers can trace the lineage.

VENDOR-NOTIFICATIONS-1.notes: NotifyVendorOnAssignment ShouldQueue + tries=4 + backoff=[30,60,300,1800] Phase-16 RESIL pattern. Auto-discovered by typed handle(TicketAssignedToVendor). Skips silently when vendor.email is null.

VENDOR-NOTIFICATIONS-2.notes: lang/{en,sw,ar}/maintenance.php new namespace with vendor_assigned.* keys in identical order across all three locales. ar/ carries [TODO-ar] placeholders per Phase 44 shrink-only baseline.

VENDOR-NOTIFICATIONS-3.notes: VendorAssignmentMailable ShouldQueue + afterCommit + locale resolution. Vendor is not a User so HasLocalePreference does not auto-fire — falls back to ticket.landlord->preferredLocale() then config(app.fallback_locale).

SLA-LANDLORD-UI-1.notes: SlaDefinitionController index/store/update/destroy under /sla (role:landlord middleware, NOT /admin/sla which is super-admin). Validates category in [issue,complaint], priority in Ticket::SLA_SECONDS keys, response/resolution bounded [60, 31536000].

SLA-LANDLORD-UI-2.notes: Pages/Sla/Index.vue table of overrides + read-only globals + create/edit modal with cascade preview (priority/category/subcategory tuple display).

SLA-LANDLORD-UI-3.notes: SlaDefinitionPolicy registered + SlaDefinitionObserver flushes the Phase-49 SlaDefinitionService::resolveFor cache on save/delete. Cache invalidation via per-landlord version-stamp key (sla:ver:{landlord|global}) embedded in resolve cache key — any write bumps version, next resolve computes fresh key, no 5-minute lag.

PARTS-REORDER-1.notes: draft_purchase_orders + draft_purchase_order_lines tables with TenantScope, soft-deletes, status enum [draft|sent|cancelled], unique(landlord_id, status, suggested_vendor_id) for open-draft-per-vendor invariant. DraftPurchaseOrder + DraftPurchaseOrderLine models with STATUS_* constants + totalEstimateCents() helper.

PARTS-REORDER-2.notes: PartsReorderSuggest cron daily 06:45 Africa/Nairobi (after parts:audit-stock 06:30, before tickets:audit-sla 07:00). Walks Part::belowThreshold; groups by inferred vendor (latest ticket_parts -> ticket.vendor_id, NULL when no history); idempotently firstOrCreate via unique index + updateOrCreate lines per part_id. Re-runs do NOT duplicate. Suggested qty = max(1, reorder_threshold * 2 - qty_available). Emits draft_purchase_orders_pending_count{landlord_id} gauge.

PARTS-REORDER-3.notes: PartsPurchaseOrderController index lists draft + sent orders with vendor + lines + estimated total. confirm() flips status=sent + sets sent_at; cancel() flips status=cancelled. Both gated by manual ownership check (cross-tenant 403). Pages/Parts/PurchaseOrders.vue cards per order with Confirm/Cancel buttons, KES formatted via Intl.NumberFormat en-KE.

COST-UI-1.notes: TicketController::show passes costs (TicketCostService::summarize) + canManageCosts props (tenant view omits costs entirely). Pages/Tickets/Show.vue sidebar Cost card after Details + Cancel sections: 4-segment proportional bar (parts/vendor/labor/other in indigo/emerald/amber/rose) + per-category breakdown formatted KES en-KE.

COST-UI-2.notes: TicketCostController::store validates category in [vendor,labor,other] (parts auto-recorded by Phase-49 TicketResolutionService::recordParts; rejected here for single source of truth), amount_cents > 0, optional notes max 500. POST /tickets/{ticket}/costs under role:landlord; writes TicketCost row + TicketActivity action=cost_recorded inside DB::transaction.

COST-UI-3.notes: TicketPolicy::createCost requires user.isLandlord() && ticket.landlord_id === user.id. Caretakers explicitly denied (they view costs but cannot mutate). TicketActivity captures category + amount_cents + currency so manual entry surfaces in audit timeline.

VENDOR-ONBOARDING-1.notes: VendorObserver::created mints 7-day URL::signedRoute(vendor.profile.edit) and queues VendorCreatedMailable. Skipped silently when vendor.email is null. VendorObserver registered in AppServiceProvider. VendorCreatedMailable ShouldQueue + afterCommit + locale fallback chain (landlord->preferredLocale -> config(app.fallback_locale)).

VENDOR-ONBOARDING-2.notes: VendorProfileController::edit + ::update under signed + throttle:invitation middleware OUTSIDE the auth group. Allow-listed mutations: contact_person, phone, address, notes. landlord_id, email, name explicitly NOT in validator so token-holder cannot pivot to identity/billing mutations. Vendor is standalone (no User row); the signed URL IS the auth.

VENDOR-ONBOARDING-3.notes: Pages/Vendor/Profile.vue standalone branded form (no AuthenticatedLayout — vendors are not logged in). Indigo->emerald gradient bg, ring-1 card. lang/{en,sw,ar}/maintenance.vendor_onboarding.* with 8 envelope keys + 8 form.* sub-keys identity-parity across locales.

CI-1.notes: Phase54MaintenanceDepth2SurfaceTest 15 tests / 30 assertions — cross-category presence map. Plus per-category watchdogs: VendorNotificationsTest 8/43, SlaLandlordUiTest 7/25, PartsReorderTest 7/22, CostUiTest 6/38, VendorOnboardingTest 7/26. Total 50 tests / 184 assertions across the cycle.

CI-2.notes: docs/runbooks/maintenance.md Phase-54 section appended with closures table (A3.1-A3.4 → commits), per-category contract details (SLA version-stamp cache invalidation, parts vendor-inference heuristic, surface-tagged cost counter-at-caller pattern from Phase 53 learning, signed-URL-as-auth + explicit allow-list for vendor onboarding), and CI gate map.

CI-3.notes: phase-49-audit-prd.json audit_closeout notes for the 4 sub-scope deferrals appended with Phase 54 commit references (NotifyVendorOnAssignment -> b996f59/ab1010b, SLA admin UI -> 0d9cb13, parts:reorder-suggest -> c704ec0, ticket cost UI -> 4f2b10c) so future readers trace the lineage without a separate index.
